//: autonomy-loop devcontainer tier (SHIPPED). A default-deny network sandbox modeled on Anthropic's claude-code reference (.devcontainer + init-firewall.sh). This is the container alternative to the srt tier for users who want a full OS boundary. It sets AUTONOMY_SANDBOX=1 so hooks/sandbox-detect.mjs reports the boundary to the preflight, runs an iptables/ipset default-deny egress firewall at start, and mounts the control plane READ-ONLY so the agent in the box cannot edit-away its own gates (the Ona finding: agents disable their own sandbox). The firewall script lives under .devcontainer/ which is mounted read-only, so the agent cannot disable it from inside.
name: autonomy-loop-sandbox
//runArgs: NET_ADMIN + NET_RAW are required for the init firewall to program iptables/ipset. They are scoped to THIS container, not the host.
//containerEnv: AUTONOMY_SANDBOX=1 is the attestation hooks/sandbox-detect.mjs reads. _TIER is for the preflight banner only.
//mounts: Mount the control plane READ-ONLY into the same workspace path. consistency=cached is fine; the point is ro. This is the container analog of srt denyWrite: the gate's own config + hooks cannot be rewritten from inside the box. Adjust the source if your repo is not at the default workspaceFolder.
//postCreateCommand: Make the firewall script executable and ensure its tooling is present.
postCreateCommand: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables ipset dnsutils aggregate jq curl && sudo chmod +x .devcontainer/init-firewall.sh
//postStartCommand: Program the default-deny firewall on every container start. Runs as root (sudo) because it touches iptables. If it FAILS, the container is NOT egress-contained: treat that as fail-closed and do not run unattended (the preflight still gates on other probes).
